search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae telehealth abdominal pain adults clinical history red flags investigations referral
site:rcem.ac.uk emergency abdominal pain documentation guideline
candidate_sources_rejected: condition-specific appendicitis, obstruction, pancreatitis, biliary, vascular, renal and early-pregnancy pathways
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: A cause-specific pathway cannot support undifferentiated abdominal-pain documentation without a clinician-established context.
Virtual assessment does not directly support manual in-person abdominal, rectal, pelvic, genital or testicular examination fields.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with undifferentiated abdominal pain. Pediatric, pregnancy-related, traumatic, postoperative, older or frail, bleeding, urological and pelvic presentations require their own qualified assessment.
setting_applicability: DHA guidance is for virtual care; only documentation domains transferable to UAE emergency assessment were mapped.
UAE_applicability: Direct UAE authority evidence, but local ED triage, emergency surgery, obstetric, imaging, laboratory, referral and disposition pathways still govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) source and exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified in the reviewed source registry and official search.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA adult abdominal-pain history section supports documenting onset, duration, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context for an adult emergency presentation; it does not diagnose the cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies onset, duration, location, character, severity, associated gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological, exposure, travel, trauma, and medical-history domains.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports recording time-critical symptoms or abnormal observations only when the clinician assessed them; it never asserts a red flag is absent.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden or severe pain, fever, vomiting, rigid abdomen, bleeding, weight loss, dysphagia, haematuria, pregnancy, and shock-associated features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording test and imaging status only when already ordered, performed or reviewed by the clinician, without recommending testing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists blood tests, urinalysis, pregnancy testing, pancreatic enzymes, and imaging selected according to the clinical assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports recording escalation, disposition, specialist discussion or handover only when the clinician made or arranged it.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists specialist and emergency referral criteria for adult abdominal pain.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The adult virtual-care scope was reviewed and limits transfer to an undifferentiated in-person ED presentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines adult abdominal-pain use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

unresolved_source_gaps: Pediatric, pregnancy, trauma, postoperative and older/frail abdominal pain; detailed pain descriptors; gastrointestinal, urinary, gynaecological and testicular domains; prior surgery/procedure; medicines; anticoagulation; examination; diagnosis; investigation selection; treatment; observation; and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
